com.jsyn.swing

Class RotaryController

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.JPanel
            • com.jsyn.swing.RotaryController
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.awt.image.ImageObserver, java.awt.MenuContainer, java.io.Serializable, javax.accessibility.Accessible

    public class RotaryController
extends javax.swing.JPanel
    Rotary controller looks like a knob on a synthesizer. You control this knob by clicking on it and dragging up or down. If you move the mouse to the left of the knob then you will have coarse control. If you move the mouse to the right of the knob then you will have fine control.

    Version:
    16.1
    Author:
    (C) 2010 Phil Burk, Mobileer Inc
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      • getModel

        public javax.swing.BoundedRangeModel getModel()

      • getFractionFromModel

        public double getFractionFromModel()
        A fractional value is useful for drawing.
        Returns:
        model value as a normalized fraction between 0.0 and 1.0

      • drawIndicator

        public void drawIndicator(java.awt.Graphics g,
                          int x,
                          int y,
                          int radius,
                          double angle)
        Override this method if you want to draw your own line or dot on the knob.

      • drawKnob

        public void drawKnob(java.awt.Graphics g,
                     int x,
                     int y,
                     int radius,
                     double angle)
        Override this method if you want to draw your own knob.
        Parameters:
        g - graphics context
        x - position of center of knob
        y - position of center of knob
        radius - of knob in pixels
        angle - in radians. Zero is straight up.
